Apollo 11

Apollo 11 was the spaceflight that first landed humans on the Moon, on July 20,
1969. The mission was crewed by Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael
Collins. Armstrong became the first person to walk on the Moon, followed by
Aldrin, while Collins remained in orbit aboard the command module. The lunar
module was named Eagle. The astronauts collected about 21.5 kilograms of lunar
material to return to Earth.
